title: zea segetalis
intro: Zea segetalis, also known as wild corn or feral corn, is a type of wild grass that belongs to the same species as domesticated corn (Zea mays). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The health benefits of Zea segetalis include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Zea segetalis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal supplements, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
